missing_main_functions: Password Reset / Forgot Password (not explicitly required but implied by security context and user experience)
Account Lockout / Brute Force Protection (not explicitly required but critical for security, especially given password complexity and verification flow)
missing_sub_functions: SUB010: Token Expiry Check on Verification Link Click (should be separate from SUB005 to explicitly test edge cases like token issued exactly 24 hours ago)
SUB011: Email Delivery Service Failure Handling during Registration (currently only covered in resend, but initial registration email delivery failure is not tested)
SUB012: Concurrent Verification Attempts (e.g., two tabs clicking the same link simultaneously to test single-use enforcement under race conditions)

unclear_categories.reason: The test intent states 'Verify that the resend functionality is only triggered when the previous token is expired or invalid, and not when a valid token still exists.' This is ambiguous because the requirement does not specify whether resend should be allowed when a valid token exists; it only says resend is available after expired/invalid link. The category may be testing an unconfirmed behavior.
Rate limiting on resend requests is mentioned in open questions and assumptions but not explicitly required. The test category assumes a behavior that is not confirmed in the requirements.

recommendations: Add a main function for 'Password Reset' to cover the full user lifecycle, even if not explicitly listed in the requirements, as it is a common companion to registration and verification.

Add a main function for 'Security and Rate Limiting' to cover brute force protection, account lockout, and rate limiting on registration and resend endpoints, which are implied by security best practices.

Add a sub-function for 'Email Delivery Failure during Registration' to ensure the system handles initial email send failures gracefully (e.g., logging error, showing generic message, not creating a record without sending email).
Add a sub-function for 'Concurrent Verification' to test race conditions when the same token is used simultaneously from multiple requests.
Clarify CAT028: either remove it if resend is only allowed after expiration/invalidation, or update the test intent to match the confirmed business rule (currently not specified).
Clarify CAT019: either remove it or add a note that it tests an assumed behavior (rate limiting) which should be confirmed with the product owner.
Consider adding a test category for 'Token Expiry Boundary' (e.g., token used exactly at 24 hours + 1 second) to ensure precise expiration handling.
Ensure that all test categories explicitly reference the requirement IDs they cover; some categories (e.g., CAT026) lack related_requirement_ids.
